text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland and Matt Norris Eleventh Sunday in Ordinary Time June 14 2026 This weekend June 13th 14th the Church celebrates the Eleventh Sunday in Ordinary Time Though we returned to Ordinary Time after Pentecost this is our first green Sunday following the Solemnities of the Holy Trinity and Corpus Christi over the past two weekends Ordinary Time is of course anything but ordinary The seasonal name itself simply reflects how these Sundays are ordinal or numbered for the remainder of the year The numbering of these Sundays reflect how long we will be in this season We are currently in the Eleventh Sunday in Ordinary Time the first several weeks having been lived between the end of Christmastide and the start of Lent This season continues now for nearly six months until the start of Advent after the Thirty fourth Sunday in Ordinary Time in November Given this length of time what is our focus to be over these next six months Up to this point we have now passed through the entire life of Christ from awaiting for the Incarnation throughout Advent to His Resurrection and Ascension before the sending of the Holy Spirit on Pentecost These next several months give us the opportunity to spend time reflecting on these great mysteries And not only to reflect but to ask how they apply to our lives What does a life transformed by the life of Christ Himself actually look like Ultimately the theme of these next six months could be described simply as The Life of Discipleship Over the coming weeks our readings will provide a roadmap for this journey Starting this weekend our Lord looks upon the crowds and His heart was moved with pity for them Mt 936 Before He sends out His Apostles He first teaches them to see others with compassion Discipleship begins by allowing our hearts to be shaped by His Sacred Heart As we move throughout the summer and fall we will hear Christ call His disciples to trust to sacrifice to forgive to pray to serve and ultimately to take up their crosses and follow Him By the time we arrive at the Solemnity of Christ the King to end the liturgical year in November we should be able to answer a simple but profound question As disciples do we allow Jesus to be our King who reigns over every aspect of our lives Entering fully into these many months of Ordinary Time may we strive to grow together in discipleship learning ever more deeply how to think with Christ love with Christ and live for Christ Commissioning of the Diocesan Synod Delegates After our 10 AM Mass this Sunday I will join with our parish delegate in traveling to Fort Wayne to take part in the training and commissioning for our upcoming diocesan Synod in November Each parish is represented by a lay delegate chosen by Bishop Rhoades I am serving as a special priest delegate given my role as a member of our diocesan Presbyteral Council which is basically a form of parish council for the Bishop Please continue to pray for the success of this important endeavor as we assist Bishop Rhoades in discerning Gods will for the future of our Diocese Totus Tuus Next weekend we will welcome our four Totus Tuus Missionaries at all of our Masses They will have the opportunity to introduce themselves to our parish and to personally invite our parish youth to join them for the Totus Tuus Summer Youth Camp The Junior High and High School Program Grades 7 12 will begin in the evening on Sunday June 21st through Thursday June 25th and the Grade School Program Grades 1 6 in the morning and afternoon on Monday June 22nd through Friday June 26th Registrations for the Totus Tuus Summer Youth Camp should be turned in this weekend though the Missionaries will take any last minute sign ups next weekend If you are interested in volunteering throughout the week please complete the volunteer forms in the Narthex As a reminder volunteers will need to be certified through our Diocesan Safe Environment training Saturday June 13 8:00am Special Intention 830 9:00am Confession 400 4:45pm Confession 5:00pm
